Deprecated API

Contents

  • Terminally Deprecated
  • Methods
  • Constructors
  • Terminally Deprecated Elements
    Element
    Description
    org.flossware.commons.io.CommonsIOException()
    since 1.32 - no production usage, provide message or cause instead
    org.flossware.commons.io.FileException()
    since 1.32 - no production usage, provide message or cause instead
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.ensureFileExists(File)
    Use FileUtil.requireExists(Path) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.ensureFileExists(String)
    Use FileUtil.requireExists(String) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.getFileInputStream(File)
    Use FileUtil.newInputStream(Path) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.getFileInputStream(String)
    Use FileUtil.newInputStream(String) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.SoapException()
    since 1.32 - no production usage, provide message or cause instead
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.ensureString(String)
    Use StringUtil.requireNonBlank(String) instead
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.ensureString(String, String)
    Use StringUtil.requireNonBlank(String, String) instead
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.fromCompressedString(String)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization. This method has critical security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.fromString(String)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization. This method has critical security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.toCompressedString(Serializable)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer serialization. This method relies on Java native serialization which has known security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.toString(Serializable)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer serialization. This method relies on Java native serialization which has known security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.UrlException()
    since 1.32 - no production usage, provide message or cause instead
  • Deprecated Methods
    Method
    Description
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.ensureFileExists(File)
    Use FileUtil.requireExists(Path) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.ensureFileExists(String)
    Use FileUtil.requireExists(String) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.getFileInputStream(File)
    Use FileUtil.newInputStream(Path) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.FileUtil.getFileInputStream(String)
    Use FileUtil.newInputStream(String) instead for modern NIO.2 API
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.ensureString(String)
    Use StringUtil.requireNonBlank(String) instead
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.ensureString(String, String)
    Use StringUtil.requireNonBlank(String, String) instead
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.fromCompressedString(String)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization. This method has critical security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.fromString(String)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization. This method has critical security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.toCompressedString(Serializable)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer serialization. This method relies on Java native serialization which has known security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
    org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil.toString(Serializable)
    Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer serialization. This method relies on Java native serialization which has known security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
